reviews.review_title: This is a good and nice VR meeting app
reviews.review_description:
Ignore the one star reviews; Either they are 12 thinking it is a game or people who think batteries are magic. 

Short version: Just try it, it is free, try the login and you will find a good VR app to brainstorm together. 

Long version: 
Yes, Glue login and inviting others is a bit of a struggle. It also still does not make sense to me using 2D whiteboards in a 3D space (especially with no feedback) 

However, the strength really lies in the inspiring environments for having idea generating conversations or collective brain storms. Also the avatars are up to par, perhaps even better, than those of Meta. I enjoy spending there to ponder and inviting others to think.
Bro fix your game its trash make some better games you dummy

There are rare social gems that smack in awesomeness…this is not that gem…it’s not even a rock…

It’s wasted space in my not installed list…

I'm using blue in Oculus Quest 2 it allows me to go and set up my avatar make rules but when it comes to me inviting my team it will not send out emails to invite my team it gives me an error message saying that email is not sent so problematically it will not allow me to create a team at all I have connected Gmail I have accepted email email confirmation for the team but it will not allow me to invite anyone I can't build a team here so for that reason my rating is a 1 right now because I have not been able to do the most fundamental aspect of the app which is to create a team to work with

developer_response.body:
Hi AbsalomAShakur,

Sorry to hear that you are experiencing issues with invitations. Have you tried to log in to web.glue.work to manage the invitations via our web portal? That is the recommended place to manage your Glue Team. If issue sustains, just reach out to us at support@glue.work and our team will help you out.

reviews.review_title: Amazing Starting Point for VR Collab
reviews.review_description:
My initial thoughts from my first meeting in Glue: 
• “This is awesome.” I spent the first 10 minutes smiling and just pressing every button available. 
• The virtual meeting rooms are beautiful, and the spatial audio is fantastic. I could tell where my co-worker was standing in the virtual room; he was able to walk into a virtual conference room and shut the door and I was no longer able to hear him. As he got closer to me, I had the natural inclination to back away. 
• After the demo ended and the sales rep left the room, we just chatted for 20 minutes. When I took the headset off, I felt like we just met in person. 
• I'd say it's a great complement to WebEx meetings and could potentially be used for virtual conferences, team meetings, engaging with customers, 1:1 brainstorming, modeling of molecules... But we might be a few years away from widespread adoption.
• After a while, I wondered: "will the novelty wear off quickly?"... but then I think of all the potential use cases for it and I'm very curious where it will all go in the coming years.

Before you get too excited, log into the Glue website and go down to the very bottom in the little tiny print and check out Pricing.  Over $70 US per person per month unless you commit for a year and save around 10% maybe?  Looks great in the video but it costs 3x as much as my Oculus for a year (!) and who knows how it really works?  Glue should at least offer a month free trial for that price.  And they are not up front about this RMR scheme.  Caveat emptor.  Deleted.

developer_response.body:
Hi VirtualD3,

Thank you for providing feedback to our app. 

Glue offers both FREE and PAID subscriptions to is remote collaboration platform. Within the app you can create a Glue Team and Glue Account with FREE subscription.

FREE subscription offers you all the features of Glue including customizable 3D avatars, spatial audio, selection of persistent meeting spaces, full suite of collaboration tools and the team files storage for your files.

With the FREE subscription, you can have up to 10 members in your Glue Team and create up to 10 Team Spaces. FREE subscription has a session time limit of 30 minutes per session.

PAID subscription options and upgrading your subscription at www.glue.work/pricing.

Hope this helps to clarify that you can use Glue for free without need to upgrade / pay.

Thanks,

Glue Team

reviews.review_description: You cannot create an account, and you can't select anything on it's menu screen. Totally unusable
It's strange how many of these Work Meet Up apps are on the Quest 2, but countless other games that would be great are not. Glue has some nice environments though, so thats fine.

Glue is where remote work is going. The environments are beautiful, the avatar animation and spatial audio really makes you feel like you are in the same room as your team mates and you have tools available to do pretty much anything you can do in a real face to face meeting - and some things you can't do. The whole package is polished and just works.

Glue is at the bleeding edge of virtual collaboration technology.

The team are consistently showcasing new features and working with their audience to push for useful functionality.

Very impressive!

I must admit after trying different VR social apps, Glue is by far the best. Good looking environments, amazing micro apps and features, nice avatars, cool lip-syncing and facial expression,... Of course there's rom to improve like defining personal boundaries to not collide with other avatar, more avatar and space personalisation, better system status visibility,... I'm going to spend more time in Glue, inviting my friends and colleagues over and run VR meetings like a boss :)

Quest really needs something like this.

A slick platform where both VR and flatscreen can meet and share. 

However...instead I found...

Unresponsive menu ‘buttons’ that just won’t work consistently , frustrating movement options (that joystick button is no good at all),  and limited functionally.

The avatar creator is not too bad...Just let me use my oculus avatar please. 

And i like having a menu button in my hand. Nice

Also — 50euros a month...per user.

I hope you nail it with Future updates , but I would hate to have to host or go to a meeting here at present.

developer_response.body:
Hi Manochang,

Thank you for providing feedback to our app. Great to see that there are many things you like about the Glue. We will continue to improve the experience with rapid cycles, so you will see the app to continuously improve and add more features.

Also, we have noticed that our description of FREE vs PAID subscriptions was not perfect on the app and we have now submitted an update to the app description to clarify it. Here is how the FREE subscription works:

Glue offers both FREE and PAID subscriptions to is remote collaboration platform. Within the app you can create a Glue Team and Glue Account with FREE subscription.

FREE subscription offers you all the features of Glue including customizable 3D avatars, spatial audio, selection of persistent meeting spaces, full suite of collaboration tools and the team files storage for your files.

With the FREE subscription, you can have up to 10 members in your Glue Team and create up to 10 Team Spaces. FREE subscription has a session time limit of 30 minutes per session.

PAID subscription options and upgrading your subscription at www.glue.work/pricing.

Hope this helps to clarify that you can use Glue for free without need to upgrade / pay.

Thanks,

Glue Team

reviews.review_description: I started using glue more than one year ago and I have to say the experience is very polished. Spaces feel open and welcoming, functionalities deliver on their promise and the new updated avatar add a new level of immersion to human interaction.

Meeting with my colleagues in Glue feels surprisingly natural - even with the stylized avatars there's a sense of real presence. Their movements and expressions really help with establishing a human connection, something we miss from the pre-pandemic days.
There is strange that it has adults only that use glue? Now it changed all ages.

I tried a few other VR platforms like this one, but this seems to be a new leader on the market.  Especially the level of immersion and avatars that look nicer than at the other software.
I'm personally impressed of this product. 5 stars!

reviews.review_title: A close to “as good as it gets”
reviews.review_description:
So happy to see this now on the Quest store and with a super usable free option! 

I benchmarked nearly every collab VR workspace that also supports users not in VR (meaning on phones and computers joining the same VR space) several months back, and this one really stood out on several levels. 

If that’s what you’re looking for as well, this in one of two I know of that delivers on getting the functionality as close to “just right” as seems possible atm, and does the core and little details of VR presence curiously well.
Having evaluated a few of the VR collaboration products, i found Glue’s virtual spaces, spatial audio, and ease of navigation the best.  This takes remote working to the next level!

ok, this app is actually really great! i got in finally and realized that my password was what was not letting me create a team. there should be more hand gestures though, such as a thumbs up, or an 👌 (ok sign) the app is actually very great, better than i thought! i love the fact that there is a laser pointer too which is cool. anyways keep on going and get this app to have 4 stars! i’m counting on you, devs!

developer_response.body:
Hi JustFrazers2021,

Great to hear you got in to Glue now and it sounds like you are already having a good time there. Gestures are actually quite topical at the moment and you will be seeing some interactive gestures coming into the platform already in April 2021. Stay tuned!

Best Regards,
Glue Team

reviews.review_description: The only reason This isn’t 4 stars is because meetings in the free subscription I feel like should be more than 30 minutes or maybe you guys should make the other subscription 30 dollars but hope you have a good day!
